LAST_DONE:    Sub-batch 5.1d Area B (AreaBoundary CUD fan-out) SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as 068d32f. 2 files changed, +241 / -4. Modified: apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/areaBoundary.ts (+57 LOC — `fanOutAreaRederive(tenantId, userId): Promise<{enqueued: number}>` private helper; queries Event + Patrol + FuelEntry via Promise.all with explicit `where: { tenantId }` defense-in-depth scoping per security.md; enqueueAreaRederive per row with entity casing "event"|"patrol"|"fuelEntry" matching AreaDerivationEntity contract from 5.1b helper; userId threaded from ctx.userId since adminProcedure guarantees it and BaseJobPayload requires it; wired into create unconditionally, into update + delete gated on `result.count > 0` to avoid spurious fan-outs on no-op writes; return shape extended from raw Prisma result to `{boundary|result, fanOut}`); apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/areaBoundary.test.ts (+188 LOC — extended prisma mock with event/patrol/fuelEntry findMany; mocked @marine-guardian/jobs for enqueueAreaRederive; updated existing create test assertion from `result.id` → `result.boundary.id` per new return shape; +6 new test cases in dedicated describe block: (1) create fans out for every Event+Patrol+FuelEntry with correct payload shape per entity, (2) create fan-out queries scoped to requesting tenant with explicit tenantId where clauses, (3) update fans out when result.count > 0, (4) update does NOT fan out when result.count === 0 (no findMany calls either), (5) delete fans out when result.count > 0, (6) delete does NOT fan out when result.count === 0). Initial typecheck flagged that BaseJobPayload requires `userId` (interface line 3) — fixed by threading ctx.userId through fanOutAreaRederive signature and updating all 5 enqueueAreaRederive calls; tests updated to assert userId in payload. NO AuditLog write — deferred to 5.1e per Option A scope split (admin manual-rebuild has full ctx.session.userId context). NO jobs package changes — 5.1d-B is consumer-side wiring only; the queue + processor + worker built in 5.1c handle the producer/consumer flow unchanged. Scope adjustment from original brief: Area A (er-sync inline re-derive on areaName change — v2 L546) deferred. Original brief assumed sync upserts compare incoming vs persisted areaName and call applyAreaDerivation on change, but ErEvent/ErPatrol interfaces in packages/jobs/src/lib/earthranger-client.ts do not currently include area_name, AND the syncEvents/syncPatrols data spreads in er-sync.processor.ts do not map any area_name field. v2 L456 says area_name should be "preserved verbatim from ER" — so Area A's precondition is an unbuilt sync-engine feature (area_name ingestion). Implementing Area A would require either bootstrapping that ingestion (out of scope for 5.1d as scoped) or implementing a phantom feature that never fires because sync never sees areaName changes. User confirmed Ship Area B Only path via AskUserQuestion. Dispatch path experience for this sub-batch: 2 consecutive Opus 4.7 subagent dispatches (ab538b0b5337b5cae, abbccff0dd9aba1b8) returned what reads as interim status messages mid-stream ("Reading the queue and area-rederive worker to verify enqueueAreaRederive signature, and verifying jobs barrel exports:" followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint; "Noted — skill suggestion ignored per [[feedback_skill_injection_hooks_ignore_when_locked_stack]]. Tech stack is locked in inputs.yml (priority 6) which overrides skill suggestions (priority 7). Continuing." followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint). Both burned ~70s + ~10 tool uses each (~287K + ~264K total_tokens reported) with zero git state changes — verified via git log/status/branch. SendMessage tool not surfaced in inventory (ToolSearch "select:SendMessage" returned "No matching deferred tools found" twice), so neither agent could be continued. User chose main-session Opus 4.7 implementation as the unblock; entire 5.1d-B work landed cleanly in ~10 minutes including governance. The dispatch-mechanism issue is the only open question — could be (a) framework-level Agent output budget that returned interim narration as terminal output, (b) PreToolUse hook noise (skill injection) that the executor interpreted as a stop signal, or (c) some Opus 4.7 subagent behavior where the model returns at natural sentence boundaries when uncertain. Worth investigating before next dispatch — but main-session is a safe fallback for cleanly-scoped sub-batches under ~30K main-session tokens.

Reason: 5.1d sync engine processor lives in @marine-guardian/jobs and apps→packages dependency direction is one-way (apps depend on packages, packages NEVER depend on apps); 5.1e admin tRPC import surface becomes `import { applyAreaDerivation } from "@marine-guardian/jobs"` via root barrel re-export. Dispatch experience: single Opus 4.7 executor (via Agent(model: "opus")) reported STATUS: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with 6 reasonable concerns: (1) helper relocation apps/web→packages/jobs architecturally required not optional — 5.1d processor in jobs needs helper, apps→packages is one-way; (2) added @marine-guardian/shared workspace dep — required by relocated helper's deriveArea import; (3) `platformPrisma as unknown as ExtendedPrismaClient` cast with explanatory comment — type-narrowing decision documented inline so 5.1d processor (next sub-batch) inherits the pattern; (4) base-worker.ts extended with optional limiter field, backward-compatible; (5) two commits collapsed via squash-merge as standard; (6) AREA_REDERIVE_CONCURRENCY=10 chosen as conservative default — v2 L545 specifies limiter not concurrency, reasoning: limiter is binding constraint (50 jobs/sec ceiling) so concurrency just prevents Redis fetch latency bottleneck. All 6 concerns sound; architect accepted without modification. Trust-but-verify pass (git log + diff stats + independent lint/typecheck/test rerun on main) confirmed accuracy. Token usage ~358K cumulative for executor across 67 tool uses (parent session pays full cost via Agent boundary on return); main-session reasoning stayed targeted via STATE.md NEXT-field driven dispatch — no full PRODUCT.md / v2 spec / governance doc reads needed because Item 1 contract is fully encoded in STATE.md ledger after 3 sub-batches. NEXT:         🎯 USER RESUME ENTRYPOINT: Open a FRESH Claude Code session and say "Start Batch 5 Item 2" (or "Continue Batch 5"). 🎯 Item 1 (AreaBoundary derivation algorithm) COMPLETE for scope under remit (5.1a + 5.1b + 5.1c + 5.1d-B + 5.1e shipped). Next concrete task is Item 2: **PatrolTrack Materialization job**. Brief shape: (a) BullMQ `patrol-track-materialize` queue + processor + worker registered in packages/jobs alongside the 5.1c area-rederive worker — same factory pattern (createPatrolTrackMaterializeWorker), conservative concurrency, rate limiter sized for ER API budget; (b) processor fetches per-patrol GPS track from EarthRanger client (extends existing er-client.ts surface), batches geometry into PatrolTrack.geometryGeojson via the stub at packages/db/src/lib/materialize-patrol-track.ts (added in Batch 4 Sub-batch 4.1b but currently a no-op stub), records source = "earthranger" + capturedAt range + recordedAt = now; (c) er-sync.processor.ts enqueues per-Patrol materialize jobs after each successful patrol upsert (idempotent — re-running for the same patrol overwrites the existing PatrolTrack row); (d) admin manual-rebuild tRPC mutation on patrol router (similar shape to 5.1e areaBoundary.rebuild — adminProcedure, optional tenantId for super_admin cross-tenant, AuditLog with PLATFORM:PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD vs PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD). Estimated ~35-45K tokens (larger than 5.1e because it spans queue/processor/worker scaffold + ER client extension + sync engine integration + tRPC mutation + UI stub). Recommended dispatch path: main-session Opus 4.7 for orientation + brief authorship + verification + governance; consider Opus 4.7 executor subagent for implementation IF dispatch-mechanism issues from 5.1d have been root-caused and resolved — otherwise main-session implementation remains the safe fallback for cleanly-scoped sub-batches. See [[project_marine_guardian_batch5_queued]] memory entry for full Batch 5 plan including Item 3 (ReportExport pdf-render queue + Puppeteer Docker service + streaming download Route Handler). After Item 3 ships, Batch 5 COMPLETE → proceed to Batch 6+ (breaking migrations + user-visible features).

DEFERRED:     5.1d Area A (er-sync inline re-derive on areaName change per v2 L546). Precondition unmet: ErEvent/ErPatrol payload types do not currently include `area_name`, and syncEvents/syncPatrols data spreads do not map any area_name field. v2 L456 says area_name should be "preserved verbatim from ER" but that ingestion has not been built. Two paths to pick up later: (1) precursor sub-batch 5.1d.A extends ErEvent + ErPatrol interfaces to include `area_name?: string | null`, adds `areaName: e.area_name ?? null` to syncEvents data spread + `areaName: p.area_name ?? null` to syncPatrols upsert data, bootstraps er-sync.processor.test.ts (no tests exist for this processor yet — significant test infrastructure work), then adds inline-derive logic; (2) fold Area A into a future Item that owns area_name ingestion as an explicit deliverable. Architect's call on which path. Either way, Area A is unblocked once area_name flows through sync.

  execution:   claude-opus-4-7 (per [[feedback_marine_guardian_opus_executor_default]] — Opus executor on all dispatched Marine-Guardian work; 5.1a + 5.1b + 5.1c confirmed three consecutive clean dispatches via this model)
